---
name: my-command
description: What this command does and when to use it
---

# My Command

Instructions for Claude to follow when this command is invoked.

1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step

---
name: fix-issue
description: Fix a GitHub issue by number
---

Fix issue #$ARGUMENTS following our coding standards
/init

CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT=true claude
/init

# In your project directory
/init

# Claude creates CLAUDE.md with structure like:
# Project Configuration
## Project Overview
- Name: Your Project
- Tech Stack: [Your technologies]
- Team Size: [Number of developers]

## Development Standards
- Code style preferences
- Testing requirements
- Git workflow conventions
# Your memory rule or instruction here

# Always use TypeScript strict mode in this project

# Prefer async/await over promise chains

# Run npm test before every commit

# Use kebab-case for file names

        C2["Scripts, templates, docs"]

    participant User
    participant Claude as Claude
    participant System as System
    participant Skill as Skill

    User->>Claude: "Review this code for security issues"
    Claude->>System: Check available skills (metadata)
    System-->>Claude: Skill descriptions loaded at startup
    Claude->>Claude: Match request to skill description
    Claude->>Skill: bash: read code-review/SKILL.md
    Skill-->>Claude: Instructions loaded into context
    Claude->>Claude: Determine:...[truncated]

my-skill/
├── SKILL.md           # Main instructions (required)
├── template.md        # Template for Claude to fill in
├── examples/
│   └── sample.md      # Example output showing expected format
└── scripts/
    └── validate.sh    # Script Claude can execute

# Basic HTTP connection
claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp

# HTTP with authentication header
claude mcp add --transport http secure-api https://api.example.com/mcp \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer your-token"

# Local Node.js server
claude mcp add --transport stdio myserver -- npx @myorg/mcp-server

# With environment variables
claude mcp add --transport stdio myserver --env KEY=value -- npx server
claude mcp add --transport sse legacy-server https://example.com/sse

modules.name: 06-hooks
topics: Try It Now: Your First Hook

examples.content: Write code → Commit → Push → CI starts → 5 min install → 10 min build → 5 min test → FAIL → Fix → Repeat
Write code → Hook runs → 2 sec → Fix immediately → Commit clean code → CI passes
mkdir -p .claude/hooks

#!/bin/bash
# Auto-format code after Write/Edit
# Receives JSON via stdin from Claude Code

INPUT=$(cat)
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')

# Skip if no file path
[ -z "$FILE_PATH" ] && exit 0

# Format based on extension
case "$FILE_PATH" in
  *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.jsx|*.json|*.md)
    prettier --write "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null && \
      echo "[Hook] Formatted: $FILE_PATH" >&2
    ;;
  *.py)
    black "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null && \
      echo "[Hook] Formatted: $FIL...[truncated]

    A -->|bundles| C
    A -->|bundles| D
    A -->|bundles| E
    A -->|bundles| F

You: Refactor the auth module to use OAuth2

Claude: [Makes 47 changes across 12 files]

You: Run the tests

Claude: Tests failed. 23 assertions broken.
       Error in payment processing.
       Database connection lost.

You: ...what just happened?

You: [Press Esc twice] Rewind to before the refactor

Claude: Restored to checkpoint "auth-oauth2-start".
       All files reverted. Conversation restored.

You: Let's try that again, one module at a time.

# Open rewind interface
/rewind

# Or use the alias
/checkpoint

flowchart TD
    A[I want to make a change] --> B{Is it risky?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Note current checkpoint ID]
    B -->|No| D[Proceed normally]
    
    C --> E[Make changes]
    D --> E
    
    E --> F{Did it work?}
    F -->|Yes| G[Continue working]
    F -->|No| H{Want to try different approach?}
    
    H -->|Yes| I[Press Esc+Esc]
    H -->|No| J[Debug the issue]
    
    I --> K[Select earlier checkpoint]
    K --> L{What to restore?}
    
    L -->|Everything| M[Restore code and conversat...[truncated]

Before: Checkpoint A (working state)
        ↓
        Changes made (broken state)
        
After:  Checkpoint A restored
        Files: Reverted
        Messages: Reverted
        Tool history: Reverted
/plan Implement user authentication system
claude --permission-mode plan

User: /plan Add a real-time notification system

Claude: I'll create a plan for implementing real-time notifications.

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Infrastructure Setup
1. Install WebSocket libraries (socket.io)
2. Set up WebSocket server
3. Configure connection management

### Phase 2: Backend Implementation
4. Create notification service
5. Implement notification queue
6. Add database schema for notifications
7. Create API endpoints

### Phase 3: Frontend Integration
8. Set up WebSocke...[truncated]

User: Modify the plan - skip the queue for now, we'll add it later

Claude: Updated plan:
[Shows modified plan with queue removed]

User: Looks good, proceed

Claude: [Implements modified plan]

# Start interactive session
claude

# Start with initial prompt
claude "explain the authentication flow"

# Single query, then exit
claude -p "what does this function do?"

# Process file content
cat error.log | claude -p "explain this error"

# Chain with other tools
claude -p "list todos" | grep "URGENT"

# Use Opus 4.6 for complex tasks
claude --model opus "design a caching strategy"

# Use Haiku 4.5 for quick tasks
claude --model haiku -p "format this JSON"

# Full model name
claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6-20250929 "review this code"

# With fallback for reliability
claude -p --model opus --fallback-model sonnet "analyze architecture"

# Use opusplan (Opus plans, Sonnet executes)
claude --model opusplan "design and implement the caching layer"
